concerns.text: Task T012a instructs generating a synthetic consent artifact to proceed if real data is missing. This violates FR-010 and Constitution Principle VI, which mandate halting the pipeline if consent documentation is missing. The task silently relaxes the mandatory halt condition by creating a synthetic bypass, weakening the ethical constraint.

Task T013a focuses exclusively on generating synthetic data without a preceding step to attempt ingestion from the verified longitudinal source (Habitica API) as required by FR-001. This drops the primary data acquisition path, effectively weakening the requirement to ingest from a verified source.

Task T021 defines the scope of Benjamini-Hochberg correction but fails to explicitly exclude time points (weeks) from the correction set. FR-007 explicitly prohibits applying correction to time points as they are repeated measures. The omission of this exclusion in the task description risks violating the statistical constraint.

Task T029 explicitly instructs 'Do not fail if variance > 0.01', which decouples the task's completion criteria from SC-004. SC-004 requires reporting a coefficient variance < 0.01 as a success criterion. The task relaxes the success condition, weakening the robustness validation requirement.

Task T031 describes varying adherence thresholds but fails to define the 'set of representative thresholds' required by SC-005. Without specifying the thresholds (e.g., specific week counts or percentages), the task allows arbitrary selection, potentially failing to satisfy the stability reporting criterion.
